page: The UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-64xCPU-512GB is a memory-optimized virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 64 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The CPU allocation is shared, and the system is configured with 64 physical cores and 1.0 thread per core. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. This server does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks show a contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance falls into the bottom 10%, while multi-core performance sits in the middle 50%. However, the server demonstrates strong capabilities in specific areas, such as a top 25% rating in PassMark CPU score, extended instructions, encryption, and physics tests. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is strong, though large block read and write bandwidths remain average. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and Geekbench ray tracing also score in the top 25%. Redis SET operations perform strongly at 67.51 million operations per second. Conversely, LLM text generation for a medium 7B model is poor, placing in the bottom 10%.

Given its shared CPU allocation and poor single-core performance, this server is less suited for latency-sensitive, single-threaded tasks. However, its high resource density of 512 GB RAM and 64 vCPUs offers qualitative cost efficiency for highly parallelized workloads that require substantial memory capacity but can tolerate shared CPU scheduling. It is well-suited for multi-threaded data processing, memory-intensive database operations, Redis caching layers, and software compilation tasks.
description: The UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-64xCPU-512GB is an x86_64 virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor, featuring 64 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz and 512.0 GB of RAM. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs. Benchmarks reveal a clear tradeoff: single-core performance is poor, ranking in the bottom 10%, whereas multi-core capabilities, PassMark CPU tests, and Redis SET operations (67.51 million operations per second) show strong performance in the top 25%. With 8.0 GB of memory per core, this server is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy, highly parallelized workloads. It is best suited for multi-threaded data processing, large-scale Redis deployments, and memory-intensive database operations.
og_description: The UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-64xCPU-512GB is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 64 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 512 GB of RAM, and no local storage, optimized for memory-intensive parallel workloads.
meta_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-64xCPU-512GB is an x86_64 virtual server with 64 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 512 GB RAM, designed for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity virtual server featuring sixty-four shared cores and half a terabyte of memory for parallelized, memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 64 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs at 2.0 GHz
512.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Strong multi-threaded decompression and Redis SET performance
No local storage or bundled GPU resources included
Best suited for memory-intensive databases and parallel data processing
